title: Top 8 Herbal Ointments for an Itchy Scalp

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole is good for an itchy scalp because it has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itchiness.
Quercetin is good for itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce redness and soothe the skin, calming itchy sensations and promoting healthy scalp.
Linalool is good for an itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation on the scalp.
Catechins are good for an itchy scalp because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the irritation, reducing the itchiness and promoting a healthy scalp.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, is a natural ingredient found in many creams and shampoos. It has been used for a long time to help with skin and scalp problems. Tea tree oil has antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to kill the germs that cause itchiness and irritation on the scalp. When applied to the scalp, it helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the skin, making it feel cooler and less itchy. It also has antiseptic properties, which help to prevent infection and promote healing. Many people find that using a cream or shampoo with tea tree oil helps to calm their itchy scalp and reduce the discomfort associated with it. The oil is easily absorbed into the skin, making it a gentle and effective treatment for itchy scalp problems.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal cream for an itchy scalp, you'll need a few simple ingredients. First, you'll need dried lavender buds, which can be found at a local health food store or online. Next, you'll need a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, which helps to moisturize and soothe the scalp. You'll also need a natural emulsifier like beeswax or shea butter, which helps to bind the ingredients together and create a smooth texture. To make the cream, start by infusing the dried lavender buds in the carrier oil for about 2 weeks, shaking the mixture every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the lavender buds. Then, melt the beeswax or shea butter in a double boiler or in the microwave, and slowly add the infused oil to the melted wax, stirring constantly until it's fully incorporated. Finally, pour the mixture into a jar or tin and let it cool and harden before using it on your scalp. When applied to the itchy scalp, the lavender oil can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy scalp.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for itchy scalp

Aloin is good for itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, soothe irritation, and calm the scalp, making it a natural remedy for relieving itchy scalp conditions.
Anthraquinones are good for an itchy scalp because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the irritated skin on the scalp, reducing the itching and discomfort.
Polysaccharides are good for itchy scalp because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itchiness and promoting a healthy scalp.
Saponins are good for itchy scalp because they have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itchiness and promoting a healthy scalp.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream is good for an itchy scalp because it helps to soothe and calm the skin. The cream is made from the gel of the aloe vera plant, which is known for its cooling and anti-inflammatory properties. When you apply the cream to your itchy scalp, it can help to reduce the redness and swelling that comes with an itchy scalp. This is because the cream has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to calm down the skin and reduce the itching. The cream also has moisturizing properties, which can help to lock in moisture and keep your scalp hydrated. This can be especially helpful if you have a dry or flaky scalp, as it can help to soothe and calm the skin. Additionally, aloe barbadensis cream is gentle and non-irritating, making it a good option for people who have sensitive skin. It can be applied directly to the itchy area, or mixed with other ingredients to create a soothing scalp treatment.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Lavandula angustifolia herbal cream for an itchy scalp, start by infusing dried lavender flowers in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Combine 1/4 cup of dried lavender flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar, and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, discarding the solids. Next, mix the infused oil with a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter to create a creamy texture. For a basic recipe, combine 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax or shea butter in a double boiler, stirring until the beeswax or shea butter melts. Remove from heat and let it cool until it reaches a thick, spreadable consistency. Transfer the cream to a clean jar and store it in a cool, dark place. To use, apply a small amount to the itchy scalp and massage gently. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of lavender will help soothe the scalp and relieve itchiness.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for itchy scalp

Quercetin is good for itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling, and also has antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the skin.

Oleanolic acid is good for itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling, and its antifungal properties help to control the growth of fungi that can cause itchiness and irritation.
Polyphenols are good for an itchy scalp because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin on the scalp, reducing itchiness and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis cream is a popular remedy for itchy scalp. The main reason it works is because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. When you have an itchy scalp, it's often caused by a fungal or bacterial infection. Rosmarinus officinalis cream helps to kill these infections, soothing the affected area and reducing the itch. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which calm the scalp and reduce redness. Additionally, the cream contains antioxidants that help to protect the scalp from damage and promote healthy hair growth. By using Rosmarinus officinalis cream, you can reduce the itch and inflammation associated with an itchy scalp, allowing your scalp to heal and feel better. The cream is also gentle and non-irritating, making it suitable for most people, even those with sensitive skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Lavandula angustifolia herbal cream for an itchy scalp, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried lavender flowers, coconut oil, beeswax, shea butter, and a few drops of lavender essential oil. Begin by infusing the dried lavender flowers in a carrier oil, such as coconut oil, by combining 1 cup of dried flowers with 2 cups of coconut oil in a heat-proof jar. Place the jar in a saucepan of simmering water, letting it steep for 2-3 hours or overnight. Once the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container. Next, melt 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl. Once the mixture is melted, stir in a few drops of lavender essential oil for added fragrance and benefits. Finally, slowly pour the infused lavender oil into the melted mixture, stirring constantly to combine. Pour the final cream into a clean container and let it cool and solidify before applying it to the itchy scalp for relief.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for itchy scalp

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica cream is a natural remedy for an itchy scalp. It contains compounds that reduce inflammation and soothe the skin. The cream is made from the leaves of the plant, which is also known as stinging nettle. When applied to the scalp, it can calm irritation and relieve redness. The anti-inflammatory properties in the cream can help to ease the itchiness caused by conditions like dandruff, eczema, or psoriasis. The cream also has antiseptic properties, which can help to prevent infections and promote healing. Additionally, Urtica dioica cream can help to balance the scalp's pH, creating an environment that's less likely to cause irritation or itchiness.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Lavandula angustifolia herbal cream that helps with an itchy scalp, start by gathering your ingredients: dried lavender flowers, coconut oil, shea butter, beeswax, and a small saucepan. Begin by infusing a cup of dried lavender flowers in two cups of coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat for about 30 minutes, then let it cool. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the lavender flowers. In a separate saucepan, melt the shea butter and beeswax together over low heat. Once melted, slowly add the lavender-infused coconut oil to the mixture and stir until well combined. Continue stirring until the mixture thickens and reaches a creamy consistency. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the cream cool completely before transferring it to an airtight container. Once the cream is cool, apply it to your itchy scalp and gently massage it in. Leave the cream on for a few hours or overnight before washing it out with shampoo. This soothing lavender cream can provide relief from an itchy scalp and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal creams for itchy scalp

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass oil, is a natural ingredient found in many creams. It's good for an itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can reduce swelling and redness caused by irritation. Lemongrass oil also has antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to get rid of the germs that can cause itchiness. When applied to the scalp, it can soothe and calm the skin, providing relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, lemongrass oil has a cooling effect, which can help to reduce heat and discomfort associated with an itchy scalp. When used regularly, Cymbopogon citratus creams can help to keep the scalp healthy and itch-free. It can also help to prevent future breakouts by keeping the scalp clean and balanced.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal cream for an itchy scalp, you will need dried lavender flowers, coconut oil, shea butter, beeswax, and a small saucepan. First, infuse the dried lavender flowers in a small bowl of coconut oil by covering the bowl with a lid and letting it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. This process will allow the lavender's healing properties to dissolve into the oil. After the infusion, strain the oil through a cheesecloth into a new container and discard the lavender flowers. Next, melt the beeswax in a saucepan over low heat, then add the infused lavender oil and shea butter to the melted beeswax. Stir the mixture until it's smooth and well combined, then pour it into a jar. Let the mixture cool and solidify before using it on your itchy scalp. When applying the cream, gently massage it into your scalp using circular motions to help soothe and calm any irritation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis cream is a natural remedy for an itchy scalp. It contains ingredients that help soothe and calm the skin. The cream has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling on the scalp. This helps to ease the discomfort and itching caused by irritation. It also has antifungal properties that fight fungal infections, which can cause itchy scalp. The cream's moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy skin. This makes it a good treatment for people who experience dryness and itchiness on their scalp due to cold weather, over-shampooing, or other factors. By using Saponaria officinalis cream, you can help to calm and soothe an itchy scalp, reducing discomfort and promoting healthy hair growth. The cream is also gentle and non-irritating, making it a safe choice for people with sensitive skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal cream for an itchy scalp, start by gathering dried lavender flowers and other ingredients like coconut oil, beeswax, and shea butter. First, infuse the dried lavender flowers in a carrier oil like coconut oil by placing them in a clean glass jar and letting it sit for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar daily. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, stirring between each interval until smooth. Then, slowly add the infused oil to the melted wax and butter mixture, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Pour the mixture into small containers or a tin, let it cool and harden, and your lavender cream is ready to use. Apply it to your scalp, massage it in, and leave it on for 30 minutes to an hour before rinsing it off with shampoo for relief from itchiness and soothing dryness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis cream is a great remedy for an itchy scalp. This cream is made from the petals of the marigold flower, which has been used for centuries to soothe skin irritations. When applied to the scalp, it helps to calm down inflammation and reduce redness. The cream also has antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help to eliminate the underlying causes of itchiness, such as fungal infections or irritation from shampoos or hair products. Additionally, calendula cream has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and ease the discomfort associated with an itchy scalp. By soothing the scalp and eliminating the causes of itchiness, calendula officinalis cream can help to provide long-lasting relief from an itchy scalp.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal cream for itchy scalp, start by preparing the dried lavender flowers. Measure 1 part of dried lavender flowers to 2 parts of carrier oil, such as coconut oil or jojoba oil, in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, gently simmering for 10-15 minutes, allowing the lavender essence to infuse into the oil. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool for a few hours or overnight. Next, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the lavender flowers. In another container, mix 1 teaspoon of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring until the beeswax is melted. Combine the lavender-infused oil with the beeswax mixture and stir well to create a smooth, thick cream. Pour the cream into a clean container, allowing it to cool and solidify before use. Apply the cream to your itchy scalp to soothe and calm the irritation, enjoying the benefits of lavender's anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia cream is good for an itchy scalp because it has soothing properties. The cream is made from the essential oil of the lavender plant, which is known for its calming effects. When you apply the cream to your scalp, the soothing properties can help reduce the itching and irritation. The cream can also help to cool down the scalp, which can be inflamed and hot when you have an itchy scalp. This cooling effect can bring relief and make you feel better. Additionally, Lavandula angustifolia cream has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and redness on the scalp. This can also help to prevent further irritation and itching. The cream can be applied directly to the affected area, and it can be left on for as long as needed to get the full benefits.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for an itchy scalp, you will need dried lavender flowers, a carrier oil, and a cream base. First, infuse the dried lavender flowers in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil by placing them in a clean glass jar and letting it sit in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks. This will help to extract the lavender's calming and antiseptic properties into the oil. Once the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the lavender flowers. Next, mix the infused oil with a cream base, such as shea butter or beeswax, in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir the mixture until it's smooth and creamy, then remove it from the heat and let it cool before transferring it to a clean container. Your lavender herbal cream is now ready to use - simply apply it to your scalp, gently massaging it in to help soothe and calm any itchiness.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for itchy scalp

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great solution for an itchy scalp. One key ingredient is Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help control scalp infections and reduce inflammation. This can provide relief from itchy sensations and soothe irritated skin. Aloe barbadensis, or aloe vera, is another helpful herb in herbal creams. It is known for its cooling and moisturizing properties, which can calm the scalp and reduce redness. Fixing an itchy scalp issue is important because it can lead to hair loss, scarring, and increased stress levels. When the scalp is itchy, it can be hard to focus on work, studies, or daily activities, and can even affect sleep quality. By using herbal creams, you can alleviate these symptoms and improve your quality of life. This can help you feel more confident, relaxed, and able to tackle daily tasks with ease.
supplementary_best: For an itchy scalp, a combination of 'Melaleuca alternifolia' and 'Aloe barbadensis' can provide effective relief. 'Melaleuca alternifolia', also known as tea tree oil, contains compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol that have antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritation on the scalp. Meanwhile, 'Aloe barbadensis', or aloe vera, is rich in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin E, which is known for its soothing and moisturizing effects. Its anti-inflammatory properties can also help calm redness and itching on the scalp. By combining these two herbs, you can create a powerful remedy that not only soothes and calms the itchy scalp but also prevents further irritation and infection.
supplementary_avoid: If you have an itchy scalp, there are some herbal creams you should avoid using. Tea tree oil-free peppermint cream can irritate the scalp further, making itching worse. This is because peppermint oil can be too cooling and drying for the skin, causing more irritation and inflammation.  Another herbal cream to avoid is one containing wintergreen oil. Wintergreen oil contains salicylic acid, which can make the itching and irritation on your scalp worse. Salicylic acid can break down the skin's natural barrier, leading to more itching and discomfort.  You should also avoid creams with ginger oil, as it can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some people. Ginger oil can be too intense for the skin, and it may make the itching on your scalp worse.  Lastly, avoid creams containing sage oil, as it can be drying and irritating to the skin. Sage oil can disrupt the skin's natural balance, leading to more itching and discomfort on the scalp.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel is a natural remedy that can help with itchy scalp. It has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the skin and reduce redness. When applied to the scalp, aloe vera gel can calm irritation and provide relief from itchiness. Apple cider vinegar can also be used to help with itchy scalp. It balances the pH of the scalp, removing dirt and oil buildup that can cause itchiness. When mixed with water and applied to the scalp, apple cider vinegar can help to reduce flaking and itchiness.  Coconut oil is another natural remedy for itchy scalp. It has moisturizing properties that hydrate the scalp, reducing dryness and itchiness. When applied to the scalp, coconut oil can also help to soothe irritation and promote healthy hair growth. Baking soda can be used to exfoliate the scalp, removing dirt and oil buildup that can cause itchiness. When mixed with water, baking soda can be applied to the scalp as a paste, helping to remove dead skin cells and reduce itchiness.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have itchy scalp often experience other skin and scalp problems. Dandruff, a condition where dead skin cells flake off, is common. This is because itchy scalp can be a sign of a fungal infection, which can also cause dandruff. To heal dandruff, people can use medicinal herbs like tea tree oil, which has antifungal properties. It helps kill the fungus that causes dandruff, reducing flaking and itching.  Eczema, a condition where the skin becomes inflamed and itchy, can also be linked to itchy scalp. Herbs like aloe vera and chamomile can help soothe the skin and reduce inflammation. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can calm the skin, while chamomile has antiseptic properties that can prevent infection.  Psoriasis, a condition where the skin becomes dry and scaly, can also cause itchy scalp. Herbs like turmeric and ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which can help reduce redness and swelling, while ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the skin.  Seborrheic dermatitis, a condition where the skin becomes inflamed and itchy, often on the scalp, face, and ears, can be treated with herbs like licorice root and sage. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and soothe the skin, while sage has antiseptic properties that can prevent infection.  These medicinal herbs can help heal itchy scalp and other related skin and scalp problems by reducing inflammation, preventing infection, and soothing the skin. They can be used in the form of oils, creams, or teas, and can be applied directly to the scalp or skin.
